replies.author: @carlrood4457
replies.text:
It dates back to a time when we just didn't see these people that often and it was a rare glimpse into a life we'd never experience.  They also were a bit more focused on movies people actually watched so there was something of a rooting interest.  In the heyday of awards shows, and when they began to proliferate on TV, you'd only see actors in their movies, in a brief interview with Johnny Carson to promote it, and very occasionally a longer Barbara Walters interview.  While they always had their causes, it wasn't as in your face and the dialog was a lot more civil.

Social media made them way too accessible and their egos didn't allow all but a few of them to keep their mouths shut.
